cover.title: THE PIONEER
cover.subtitle: The one who moves first, and forgets to ask who is moving with them.
cover.tagline: You light the fire. What you're learning now is how not to carry it alone.

profile_scores.caption: High drive and expression, low stability — you move first, but rarely stop to check who is moving beside you.

short_summary: You see possibility quickly and act on it before most people do.
The exhaustion wasn't from leading. It was from leading alone.
What you're learning next is how to wait for someone to move toward you too.

full_report.headline: THE PIONEER
full_report.intro: You move first, decide quickly, and create momentum wherever you go. This report explores why you keep becoming the engine of the relationship, what that costs you, and how to recognize people who move on their own.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're not someone who waits long before acting. When you see possibility, you move.
People see confidence. What they rarely see is how little space you leave yourself to hesitate.
Your strength is initiative. Your risk is assuming movement means mutual commitment.
chapters.profile_note: High Drive · High Expression, Low Stability — You create momentum, but rarely pause to see who shares it.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Keep Repeating
body: You make plans, suggest directions, and keep the relationship moving.
Eventually you realize that if you stop pulling, everything stops with you.
chapters.highlight: The exhaustion wasn't from leading. It was from never being led toward, even once.

chart.title: THE EXHAUSTION CURVE
chart.description: Momentum carries the relationship until exhaustion suddenly replaces it.

chapters.title: What You've Gotten Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: You do hesitate. You simply move before the hesitation finishes. What feels like personality is often an old habit of covering uncertainty with action.
items.truth_short: You hesitate too—you've just learned to outrun it.

items.belief: If I move first, they'll follow.
items.truth: People decide at their own pace. Following you and choosing with you are different things.
items.truth_short: Movement isn't the same as commitment.

items.belief: I got hurt because I approached first.
items.truth: Approaching wasn't the problem. Skipping confirmation was.
items.truth_short: The issue wasn't speed. It was failing to check together.

items.belief: Deciding fast has never cost me anything.
items.truth: What hurt most wasn't loving the wrong person. It was becoming certain too early.
items.truth_short: You suffered more from certainty than from love itself.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You're drawn to people who are still deciding, still searching, still becoming.
Their uncertainty makes your certainty feel meaningful.
chapters.highlight: Someone who follows you is different from someone who chooses you.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: You don't need someone impressed by your momentum.
You need someone who keeps moving even after you stop.
chapters.action_sentence: In the right relationship, you're not the only person creating direction.

chapters.title: Where You Go From Here
body: You already know how to begin.
Now you're learning how to trust stillness without assuming abandonment.
chapters.transition_note: Leading is a strength. Carrying everything alone doesn't have to be.

chapters.title: The Types You Keep Falling For

items.type_title: The Person Who Hasn't Decided Yet
items.description: You see possibility before they do and begin choosing for both of you.

items.type_title: The Person Who Only Reacts to Your Energy
items.description: They move when you move and stop when you stop.

items.type_title: The Person Who Loves Your Certainty
items.description: They admire your confidence but struggle when you hesitate.

items.type_title: The Person Who Envies Your Drive
items.description: They watch your momentum without creating their own.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: The signs were always present. Leading simply felt natural.

signal_strength.label: You decide most plans and directions early on
You control the pace and future of the relationship
If you stop reaching out, silence follows

chapters.core_signal: If your absence creates complete stillness, you already know the answer.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like for You

checklist.title: The relationship moves without your effort.
checklist.description: You can follow sometimes and nothing collapses.

checklist.title: They stay when you hesitate.
checklist.description: Your uncertainty doesn't scare them away.

checklist.title: They love you, not your momentum.
checklist.description: They remain even when you're not performing strength.

checklist.title: Shared decisions feel lighter.
checklist.description: Partnership reduces loneliness instead of increasing it.

chapters.title: What Happens In Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: Someone who must control everything themselves eventually collides with you.
sections.who_steadies_you: Someone who joins your direction but remains present when you pause creates balance.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You'll still notice potential quickly. The attraction itself isn't the problem.
sections.next_signs: Watch whether they move on their own within the first few weeks, not only when invited.
sections.try_this: Don't initiate once. Let silence exist and observe who creates movement without being pulled.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to begin relationships. The next step is learning not to carry them alone.
action_items: Wait once before making the next move and see who steps forward.
Ask whether someone's actions match the potential you see in them.
Practice sharing decisions instead of deciding everything first.

closing.message: You've spent years learning how to move first. This report explored how to recognize who truly moves with you.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
